response:
module 1: identify the initial number of power punches  
the boxer landed 120 power punches in the first match.  

module 2: calculate the number of power punches in the second match  
the boxer increased his number of power punches by 25% in the second match.  
25% of 120 is 30, so the boxer landed 120 + 30 = 150 power punches in the second match.  

module 3: calculate the number of power punches in the third match  
the boxer managed to land 30 more power punches than he did in the second match.  
so the boxer landed 150 + 30 = 180 power punches in the third match.  

module 4: calculate the total number of power punches across all three matches  
the boxer landed 120 power punches in the first match.  
he landed 150 power punches in the second match.  
he landed 180 power punches in the third match.  
the total number of power punches across all three matches is 120 + 150 + 180 = 450.
